concept: Live / Be Alive / Dwell in Life
description: To experience the state of being alive—breathing, sensing, changing, participating in the web of existence. 'Liona' encompasses both the biological fact of living and the richer sense of dwelling fully in one's life. It is not mere survival but engaged presence in the world.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'l', the liquid consonant of life flowing—continuous, unbroken, moving forward. The bright 'i' sparks with vitality and awareness. Then 'o' opens into fullness, life expanding into its container. Finally 'na' grounds with nasal stability, life rooted in the present moment.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an intransitive verb: 'mi liona' (I live). Can take locative phrases for dwelling: 'mi mua home liona' (I live in home). Distinct from nio (be) in emphasizing active living rather than mere existence.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Living mindfully means being present to each moment of existence, not merely passing through life but inhabiting it fully.
pillars.solarpunk-values: To live well requires sustainable relationship with the systems that support life—air, water, soil, community.
